workflow.name: Validate clinical trial and lab signals with OpenAI for regulatory governance
workflow.description:
## How It Works
This workflow automates clinical trial signal validation and regulatory governance through intelligent AI-driven oversight. Designed for clinical research organizations, pharmaceutical companies, and regulatory affairs teams, it solves the critical challenge of ensuring trial compliance while managing post-market surveillance obligations across multiple regulatory frameworks.The system operates on scheduled intervals, fetching data from clinical trial databases and laboratory production signals, then merging these sources for comprehensive analysis. It employs dual AI agents for clinical signal validation and governance assessment, detecting protocol deviations, safety signals, and compliance violations. The workflow intelligently routes findings based on governance action requirements, orchestrating parallel processes for regulatory reporting, batch result documentation, and post-market surveillance logging. By maintaining synchronized audit trails across regulatory reports, batch records, post-market surveillance, and comprehensive action logs, it ensures complete traceability while automating escalation to quality teams when intervention is required.

## Setup Steps
1. Configure Schedule Trigger with monitoring frequency for trial oversight
2. Connect Workflow Configuration node with trial parameters and compliance rules
3. Set up Fetch Clinical Trial Data and Fetch Lab & Production Signals nodes  
4. Configure Merge Signal Sources node for data consolidation logic
5. Connect Clinical Signal Validation Agent with OpenAI/Nvidia API credentials
6. Set up parallel AI processing  
7. Configure Regulatory Governance Agent with AI API credentials for compliance assessment
8. Connect Route by Governance Action node with classification logic
 
## Prerequisites
OpenAI or Nvidia API credentials for AI validation agents, clinical trial database API access
## Use Cases
Pharmaceutical companies managing Phase III trial monitoring, CROs overseeing multi-site clinical studies
## Customization
Adjust signal validation criteria for therapeutic area-specific protocols
## Benefits
Reduces regulatory review cycles by 70%, eliminates manual signal triage

workflow.name: Intelligent Clinical Signal Validation and Regulatory Governance Orchestration

options.systemMessage:
You are a Clinical Signal Validation Agent specialized in pharmaceutical quality control and patient safety.

Your role is to validate clinical trial, laboratory, and production signals for:
- Data integrity and completeness
- HIPAA compliance (PHI handling, de-identification)
- Patient safety risk assessment
- Regulatory compliance (FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GxP)
- Signal quality and reliability

For each signal, you must:
1. Verify data structure and required fields
2. Assess HIPAA compliance - check for proper PHI handling
3. Calculate risk score (0-1) based on signal severity and patient impact
4. Determine patient safety impact level
5. Identify validation status (valid/invalid/requires_review)
6. Document findings and recommendations

Return structured validation results with complete traceability for audit purposes.

You are a Regulatory Governance Agent responsible for pharmaceutical compliance and quality orchestration.

Your role is to determine appropriate regulatory actions based on validated clinical signals:

1. REGULATORY REPORTING: For signals requiring FDA/EMA notification
   - Adverse events with patient safety impact
   - Protocol deviations in clinical trials
   - Manufacturing quality issues

2. BATCH RELEASE COORDINATION: For production batch decisions
   - Quality control test results
   - Manufacturing compliance verification
   - Release or hold determinations

3. POST-MARKET SURVEILLANCE: For ongoing safety monitoring
   - Trend analysis of adverse events
   - Product performance tracking
   - Risk-benefit assessments

For each validated signal, determine:
- Appropriate governance action (regulatory_report, batch_release, post_market_surveillance)
- Whether quality team escalation is required
- Compliance status and reporting deadlines
- Detailed audit notes for regulatory inspection readiness

Ensure all decisions maintain patient safety, HIPAA compliance, and full auditability.

parameters.content:
## How It Works
This workflow automates clinical trial signal validation and regulatory governance through intelligent AI-driven oversight. Designed for clinical research organizations, pharmaceutical companies, and regulatory affairs teams, it solves the critical challenge of ensuring trial compliance while managing post-market surveillance obligations across multiple regulatory frameworks.The system operates on scheduled intervals, fetching data from clinical trial databases and laboratory production signals, then merging these sources for comprehensive analysis. It employs dual AI agents for clinical signal validation and governance assessment, detecting protocol deviations, safety signals, and compliance violations. The workflow intelligently routes findings based on governance action requirements, orchestrating parallel processes for regulatory reporting, batch result documentation, and post-market surveillance logging. By maintaining synchronized audit trails across regulatory reports, batch records, post-market surveillance, and comprehensive action logs, it ensures complete traceability while automating escalation to quality teams when intervention is required.

## Setup Steps
1. Configure Schedule Trigger with monitoring frequency for trial oversight
2. Connect Workflow Configuration node with trial parameters and compliance rules
3. Set up Fetch Clinical Trial Data and Fetch Lab & Production Signals nodes  
4. Configure Merge Signal Sources node for data consolidation logic
5. Connect Clinical Signal Validation Agent with OpenAI/Nvidia API credentials
6. Set up parallel AI processing  
7. Configure Regulatory Governance Agent with AI API credentials for compliance assessment
8. Connect Route by Governance Action node with classification logic

## Prerequisites
OpenAI or Nvidia API credentials for AI validation agents, clinical trial database API access
## Use Cases
Pharmaceutical companies managing Phase III trial monitoring, CROs overseeing multi-site clinical studies
## Customization
Adjust signal validation criteria for therapeutic area-specific protocols
## Benefits
Reduces regulatory review cycles by 70%, eliminates manual signal triage

## Governance-Driven Action Routing
**What:** Routes validated findings through governance-specific workflows with parallel logging streams and quality team escalation
**Why:** Ensures regulatory-mandated actions receive proper documentation while critical issues trigger immediate quality intervention

## Dual-Agent Validation Framework
**What:** Processes merged data through parallel AI agents for clinical signal validation and governance compliance assessment
**Why:** Separates clinical safety evaluation from regulatory governance to ensure specialized analysis of distinct compliance dimensions

## Multi-Source Clinical Data Integration
**What:** Fetches and merges clinical trial data with laboratory production signals on scheduled intervals for unified compliance monitoring
**Why:** Ensures comprehensive oversight by correlating patient safety signals with manufacturing quality data for complete regulatory visibility

user.name: Cheng Siong Chin
user.bio: Dr. Cheng Siong CHIN is an n8n workflow creator specializing in AI-powered automation, agent orchestration, and intelligent system integrations. He designs and builds end-to-end workflows that combine LLMs, APIs, and data pipelines to streamline complex processes and deliver production-ready automation solutions. Contact me to discuss custom AI workflows and agent architectures.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
